EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An arctic air mass caused strong wind and damage to portions of northwest Montana, while southwest Montana experienced a flash-freeze with heavy snow bands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A power company in northwest Montana reported infrastructure damage including a dozen poles along the western foothills of the Whitefish Range between Ksanka Peak and Marston Mountain. They estimated 40 to 60 mph wind gusts brought the fir and pine trees down. They also remarked that the strong winds swirled from the northerly direction, which probably contributed to the tree damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An arctic air mass caused strong wind and damage to portions of northwest Montana, while southwest Montana experienced a flash-freeze with heavy snow bands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rain made roads wet before freezing up when the arctic air moved west across the Continental Divide. Snow on top of this freeze created icy conditions which contributed to several vehicle accidents on Interstate 90. Several snow bands brought white-out conditions from snow and gusty winds which worsened travel and road conditions. Highway 1 between Drummond and Georgetown Lake were also affected by ice, as reported by MDT.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An arctic air mass caused strong wind and damage to portions of northwest Montana, while southwest Montana experienced a flash-freeze with heavy snow bands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A person from the public sent in a photo of Interstate 90 west of Bearmouth which portrayed icy conditions. They said they saw several roll-overs west of Drummond on their way to Missoula. A news report in the evening reported multiple vehicles in ditches between Turah and Clinton after a passing snow band caused white-out conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An arctic front moved through the Billings Forecast Area. At the same time, abundant moisture off the Pacific moved over this very cold airmass, resulting in what is called an overrunning event. As a result, heavy snow occurred across the entire Billings Forecast Area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of 6 inches were reported across the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flooding occurred across many areas of south central and southeast Montana during the latter portions of the month.  A considerable snow cover existed through the month. Surface and sub-surface ground temperatures remained frozen through the last week of March. Warming temperatures resulted in this snow to quickly melt. Widespread flooding occurred due to the snow melt not being able to soak into the ground. In addition, the warm temperatures allowed river ice to break loose resulting in ice jams along a few rivers. Many creeks and ditches filled with water with some roads being washed out. Although some outbuildings and a house were flooded, most of the flooding occurred over open ranch land.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding due to an ice jam was reported on the Yellowstone River east of Amelia Island just north of Hysham.  Low-land flooding along the river and adjacent farm fields was reported. One home experience some flooding. Otherwise, no major impacts were reported with this ice jam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flooding occurred across many areas of south central and southeast Montana during the latter portions of the month.  A considerable snow cover existed through the month. Surface and sub-surface ground temperatures remained frozen through the last week of March. Warming temperatures resulted in this snow to quickly melt. Widespread flooding occurred due to the snow melt not being able to soak into the ground. In addition, the warm temperatures allowed river ice to break loose resulting in ice jams along a few rivers. Many creeks and ditches filled with water with some roads being washed out. Although some outbuildings and a house were flooded, most of the flooding occurred over open ranch land.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Significant impacts occurred across portions of Big Horn County due to a deep snow cover melting on frozen ground, as well as an ice jam on the Little Big Horn River. Due to the ground being frozen, the water from the melting snow could not soak into the ground. This resulted in many areas flooding. Although most of the flooding occurred across open range land, some flooding did impact roads, some homes, and commerce. |Impacts occurred mainly from the ice jam on the Little Big Horn River and its tributaries which resulted in stranding some people in their homes. In addition, flooding from the Little Big Horn River inundated Interstate 90 at exit 510 at Crow Agency across both the east and west bound lanes. The interstate was closed from Hardin to the Wyoming line for several days. Highway 212 had water over it as well. These closures resulted in significant impacts to commerce. The Crow Tribe Executive Branch declared a State of Emergency. The Red Cross set up a shelter in Hardin for those affected by the Little Big Horn River flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An arctic front moved through the Billings Forecast Area. At the same time, abundant moisture off the Pacific moved over this very cold airmass, resulting in what is called an overrunning event. As a result, heavy snow occurred across the entire Billings Forecast Area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of 6 inches were reported across the area.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread flooding occurred across the eastern and southern portions of Powder River County.  Many rural areas flooded with rivers and creeks flooding some homes. The Powder River and Little Powder River had water out of their banks. Barns and sheds in low-lying areas flooded. Many gravel and back roads flooded and were washed out, resulting in many people not being able to leave their homes.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Many roads along the foothills were washed out or severely damaged due to rain and snowmelt on top of ground that remained frozen. Approximately 12 roads were damaged, 9 of which had to be closed. The county applied for a disaster declaration due to the extent of the damage. Damage amounts totaled $804,387.00.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper low moved across Wyoming during the 12th and 13th. This low provided low-level moisture to advect into southeast Montana resulting in heavy snow. In addition, tight surface pressure gradients associated with the low resulted in very gusty winds. Blizzard conditions occurred across the area resulting in severe driving conditions and some highway closures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibilities of less than a 1/4 mile resulted in blizzard conditions across the southern portions of the county. Severe driving conditions were reported on Highways 212 and 323.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain over the Big Horn Mountains resulted in significant runoff into the Tongue River. The river crested just over 11 feet which was 4 feet higher than the record crest of 7.3 feet set in 2011. Flooding occurred along the Tongue River from the Birney area to just southeast of Colstrip.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain over the Big Horn Mountains resulted in significant runoff into the Tongue River. The river crested just over 11 feet which was 4 feet higher than the record crest of 7.3 feet set in 2011. Flooding occurred along the Tongue River from the Birney area to just southeast of Colstrip. |One home was inundated by the flood waters. A levee in Ashland flooded at St Labre which resulted in  homes in the Cheyenne Village near St Labre being accessed only by boat for a period of time. Water inundation of these homes was confined to crawl spaces and not up to floor level. Six homes on the Northern Cheyenne Tribe Reservation were inundated by floodwaters. The American Red Cross set up a shelter at the Ashland Public School. |The Tongue River was also out of its banks at Brandenberg, mainly in the grassy areas, but it did cover about 3 feet of the gravel on Tongue River Road.
